fields.question: How can I join the Moto Club?
fields.answer: You can join by completing the online registration form on our website, or by visiting the club premises during opening hours (Wednesday and Friday 20:30–23:00, Saturday 15:00–18:00). A valid photo ID and payment of the membership fee are required.

fields.question: How much does a membership card cost?
fields.answer:
We offer three membership tiers:

  • Standard Membership: €50/year – voting rights, events, gallery uploads
  • Supporter Membership: €30/year – events, 10% discount
  • Premium Membership: €100/year – all privileges, 20% event discount

fields.question: Can I participate in events without being a member?
fields.answer: Some events are also open to non-members (such as the Ride for Children). For most club-organised events a valid membership card is required. Non-members may attend as guests for a maximum of 2 outings before being required to register.

fields.question: How does mutual aid work?
fields.answer: The mutual aid system allows members to request and offer assistance in case of a breakdown or roadside emergency. Each member can indicate their skills (mechanics, transport, logistics) and their availability radius on their profile. When help is needed the system notifies available members in the area.

fields.quote: Joining this club completely changed the way I experience riding. The Sunday rides and rallies are truly unforgettable moments.
The mutual support among members is extraordinary. Once I broke down on the Stelvio pass, and within 30 minutes help was already on its way.
The club-organised tours are always flawless: breathtaking routes, great food stops and wonderful company.

fields.title: Moto Club Aquile Rosse announces the 2026 events calendar
fields.body:
Moto Club Aquile Rosse ASD of Bergamo has presented the official events calendar for the 2026 season, featuring over 50 appointments including rallies, tours, track days and charitable initiatives.

Highlights include: the Season Opener at Mandello del Lario, the Orobie Mountain Tour, the Track Day at Franciacorta Circuit and the 12th edition of the Ride for Children in aid of Papa Giovanni XXIII Hospital.

Press contact: press@aquilerosse.it

fields.title: Ride for Children 2025: €8,500 raised for Paediatrics
fields.body:
The 11th edition of the Ride for Children — the annual charity ride of Moto Club Aquile Rosse — concluded with great success. The event drew more than 200 riders and raised €8,500, donated entirely to the Paediatrics ward of Papa Giovanni XXIII Hospital in Bergamo.

Since 2014 the Ride for Children has raised a cumulative total of over €43,000.

fields.description: Vintage motorcycle with a broken gearbox on the way back from the Mandello rally. Need a van to transport the bike to the club premises in Bergamo (~60 km).
Returning from the Dolomites tour with a chain problem on my V85 TT near Treviglio. The bike moves slowly but I can't make another 80 km to Lecco tonight. Looking for a garage and a place to sleep.
Motorcycle broken down on the SS38 between Lecco and Colico, km 42. The bike won't start — looks like an electrical fault. I'm in the car park of Ristorante La Pergola.
Rear tyre puncture on my motorcycle on the Passo della Presolana road. I don't have a tubeless repair kit. I'm at Rifugio Magnolini.
Run out of fuel on the SP671 between Clusone and Bergamo. The nearest petrol station was closed (Sunday evening). I'm at Bar Sport in Gazzaniga.

fields.notes: Trasporto moto con carrello. Disponibile h24.
Meccanico esperto, disponibile con furgone attrezzato.
Carro attrezzi per moto. Disponibile weekend.

fields.title: My first ride with the club
fields.body: It was a Saturday in April 2019 when I went on my first ride with Moto Club Red Eagles. I didn't know anyone, I was nervous. But all it took was stopping at the first café to realise these were my people. Since that day I have never missed a ride.

fields.title: The Stelvio solo: fear and wonder
fields.body: Last year I decided to tackle the Stelvio on my own. 48 hairpins, sudden fog, and halfway up a pressure drop in the rear tyre. I called mutual aid and in 30 minutes Alessandro was there with the van. That is what this club is about.

fields.title: Proposal: 5-day Sardinia Tour
fields.body: I propose a 5-day tour in Sardinia for September 2026. Route: Olbia → Costa Smeralda → Barbagia → Cagliari → Oristano → Alghero → Porto Torres. I have already contacted 3 biker-friendly B&Bs along the route. Estimated budget: €600 per person all inclusive (ferry, accommodation, breakfasts).

fields.title: Membership renewal: deadline 31 March
fields.body: Reminder to all members that the deadline for renewing your 2026 membership card is 31 March. Members who renew before 15 March will receive a 10% discount. You can renew online from your personal profile or in person at the clubhouse during opening hours.

fields.title: 40 years in the saddle: my collection
fields.body: I started collecting motorcycles in 1987, the year the club was founded. Today my garage holds 7 bikes: a Le Mans III from '81, a Stornello Sport from '68, a V7 Classic, a California 1400, a V85 TT, a V100 Mandello and a Griso 1200. Each one has a story, each one has taken me to incredible places.

fields.content: Great news! Mandello is always a must-attend event. Who's coming with the group from Bergamo?
I'll be there! Who wants to join me for the lake loop in the afternoon?
Present as every year. I'm bringing the Le Mans III too — should make an impression in the vintage bike area.
Tuscany on a motorbike is a dream. I'm already planning the trip with Giulia.
The Bayern Treffen was fantastic. The roads of Franconia are among the best in Europe.
Registered! Does anyone know a good hotel in Mandello?
The Orobie mountains on a bike are spectacular. Beautiful route.

content.intro:
Moto Club Aquile Rosse was founded in 1987 in Bergamo by a group of passionate motorcyclists who wanted to share their love of two wheels in a spirit of friendship and solidarity.

Today the club has over 250 registered members, with headquarters at Via Borgo Palazzo 22, Bergamo. We are affiliated with the FMI (Italian Motorcycling Federation) and organise more than 50 events each year including rallies, tours, track days and charity initiatives.

content.faq:
[{"type": "faq", "value": {"title": "Frequently Asked Questions about the Federation", "items": [{"question": "What is the club federation?", "answer": "

It is a voluntary network of clubs that share events and initiatives through a secure protocol. Every club retains full autonomy.

"}, {"question": "Do I need to pay to access federated events?", "answer": "

No. Access to the partner events list is included in your membership. Registering for a partner event follows the rules of the organising club.

"}, {"question": "Is my personal data shared?", "answer": "

No. The federation exchanges only event data and anonymous aggregate counts. No personal data is ever transmitted to partner clubs.

"}, {"question": "How is security guaranteed?", "answer": "

Each partner club has unique API keys. All communications use HTTPS with HMAC authentication. Data is validated and sanitised before import.

"}, {"question": "Can I attend events at a partner club?", "answer": "

You can express interest from our platform. For actual registration, follow the instructions on the organising club's website via the link on the event page.

"}, {"question": "Are comments visible to partner clubs?", "answer": "

No. Comments on partner events are visible only to members of our own club. They are a tool for internal discussion.

"}]}, "id": "2cd55573-63ad-40fb-862f-4c4e89a0711c"}]

[{"type": "rich_text", "value": "

Benefits of the Federation

Being part of a federated network of clubs means more opportunities for members without losing autonomy:

  • More events \u2014 Access a shared calendar with dozens of appointments from partner clubs.
  • New friendships \u2014 Meet enthusiasts from other clubs who share your interests.
  • Privacy guaranteed \u2014 Your personal data stays within our club.
  • Private discussion \u2014 Comment and organise with your club's members in complete privacy.

The federation is an open project: any club can join by following the technical protocol and the principles of transparency and privacy.

", "id": "c1a82631-02f2-44b1-a7d7-4c6144a4a06a"}]

Last weekend a group of 8 members from Moto Club Aquile Rosse crossed the Alps to attend the Spring Franken Bayern Treffen, the spring rally that marks the start of the season in the German region of Franconia.

The outbound route passed through the Brenner Pass, Innsbruck and Munich before reaching the Nuremberg area. The rally, attended by over 500 riders from across Europe, offered days of riding on the magnificent roads of Franconia, Bavarian beer and a wonderful spirit of camaraderie.

Next international trip: moto Days 2026 in South Africa!

", "id": "330fb35d-0017-485b-a590-271940d0f7a6"}]

fields.object_str: Getting Your Bike Ready for the Season

content.body:
[{"type": "rich_text", "value": "

With the warmer months approaching it's time to prepare your bike for the first rides. Here is an essential checklist prepared with our technical partner Officina Moto Bergamo.

Essential Checks

  1. Battery: Check the charge and electrolyte levels. If the bike has been stored for more than 3 months, consider replacement.
  2. Tyres: Check pressure, tread depth and the DOT date. Tyres older than 5 years should be replaced.
  3. Brakes: Check pad thickness and brake fluid level.
  4. Engine oil: Change oil and filter if not done before winter storage.
  5. Chain: Clean, lubricate and check tension.
  6. Lights: Test all lights including indicators and number-plate light.
", "id": "8bcedde9-b03f-4e97-9112-1a7d934d09b9"}]

Our History

Founded as a small group of friends meeting every Sunday for a ride on the roads around Bergamo, the club has grown into one of the most active in Lombardy.

Our Values

  • Passion: Motorcycling is our way of life
  • Safety: Responsible riding and continuous training
  • Solidarity: Mutual aid among members
  • Inclusivity: All makes and models are welcome

content.intro:
Moto Club Aquile Rosse was founded in 1987 in Bergamo by a group of passionate motorcyclists who wanted to share their love of two wheels in a spirit of friendship and solidarity.

Today the club has over 250 registered members, with headquarters at Via Borgo Palazzo 22, Bergamo. We are affiliated with the FMI (Italian Motorcycling Federation) and organise more than 50 events each year including rallies, tours, track days and charity initiatives.

As every year, the riding season kicks off with the traditional Season Opener at Mandello del Lario, the birthplace of Moto moto.

The event, organised by Moto Club Le Aquile di Mandello in collaboration with moto Days, features a packed programme: from the lakeside parade to guided tours of the Moto moto Museum, culminating in a club dinner at Ristorante Il Griso.

Programme

  • 09:00 \u2013 Meet at Piazza Garibaldi
  • 10:30 \u2013 Parade along Lake Como
  • 12:30 \u2013 Club lunch
  • 15:00 \u2013 Motorcycle museum visit
  • 18:00 \u2013 Aperitif and prize-giving

Moto Club Aquile Rosse will attend with a delegation of 30 members. Registrations are open until 15 March.

"}]
content.intro: The traditional season-opening rally returns to Mandello del Lario with surprises in store for all motorcycle enthusiasts.
content.title: Season Opener 2026: Back to Mandello!

content.body:
[{"id": "57993ec3-aaaa-4edd-8294-d01675e088ec", "type": "rich_text", "value": "

Exciting news from the Italian rally scene: the 1st Moto moto Lands of Pisa has been announced, an event to be held at Pontedera (PI) in spring 2026.

Organised by Moto Club Terre di Pisa in collaboration it promises a weekend steeped in Tuscan motorcycling culture: guided rides through the Pisan hills, tastings of local produce and a static display in the historic town centre.

Our club is already planning a group trip. Interested members should contact events manager Davide Marchetti.

"}]

[{"id": "330fb35d-0017-485b-a590-271940d0f7a6", "type": "rich_text", "value": "

Last weekend a group of 8 members from Moto Club Aquile Rosse crossed the Alps to attend the Spring Franken Bayern Treffen, the spring rally that marks the start of the season in the German region of Franconia.

The outbound route passed through the Brenner Pass, Innsbruck and Munich before reaching the Nuremberg area. The rally, attended by over 500 riders from across Europe, offered days of riding on the magnificent roads of Franconia, Bavarian beer and a wonderful spirit of camaraderie.

Next international trip: moto Days 2026 in South Africa!

"}]
content.intro: Eight club members took part in the spring rally in Bavaria. Here is the story of their adventure.
content.title: Recap: Spring Franken Bayern Treffen 2026

fields.object_str: Getting Your Bike Ready for the Season

content.body:
[{"id": "8bcedde9-b03f-4e97-9112-1a7d934d09b9", "type": "rich_text", "value": "

With the warmer months approaching it's time to prepare your bike for the first rides. Here is an essential checklist prepared with our technical partner Officina Moto Bergamo.

Essential Checks

  1. Battery: Check the charge and electrolyte levels. If the bike has been stored for more than 3 months, consider replacement.
  2. Tyres: Check pressure, tread depth and the DOT date. Tyres older than 5 years should be replaced.
  3. Brakes: Check pad thickness and brake fluid level.
  4. Engine oil: Change oil and filter if not done before winter storage.
  5. Chain: Clean, lubricate and check tension.
  6. Lights: Test all lights including indicators and number-plate light.
"}]
content.intro: Our trusted mechanic's tips for bringing your motorcycle back to peak condition after the winter break.
content.title: Getting Your Bike Ready for the Season
content.draft_title: Getting Your Bike Ready for the Season

[{"id": "2cd55573-63ad-40fb-862f-4c4e89a0711c", "type": "faq", "value": {"items": [{"answer": "

It is a voluntary network of clubs that share events and initiatives through a secure protocol. Every club retains full autonomy.

", "question": "What is the club federation?"}, {"answer": "

No. Access to the partner events list is included in your membership. Registering for a partner event follows the rules of the organising club.

", "question": "Do I need to pay to access federated events?"}, {"answer": "

No. The federation exchanges only event data and anonymous aggregate counts. No personal data is ever transmitted to partner clubs.

", "question": "Is my personal data shared?"}, {"answer": "

Each partner club has unique API keys. All communications use HTTPS with HMAC authentication. Data is validated and sanitised before import.

", "question": "How is security guaranteed?"}, {"answer": "

You can express interest from our platform. For actual registration, follow the instructions on the organising club's website via the link on the event page.

", "question": "Can I attend events at a partner club?"}, {"answer": "

No. Comments on partner events are visible only to members of our own club. They are a tool for internal discussion.

", "question": "Are comments visible to partner clubs?"}], "title": "Frequently Asked Questions about the Federation"}}]
content.body:
[{"id": "c1a82631-02f2-44b1-a7d7-4c6144a4a06a", "type": "rich_text", "value": "

Benefits of the Federation

Being part of a federated network of clubs means more opportunities for members without losing autonomy:

  • More events \u2014 Access a shared calendar with dozens of appointments from partner clubs.
  • New friendships \u2014 Meet enthusiasts from other clubs who share your interests.
  • Privacy guaranteed \u2014 Your personal data stays within our club.
  • Private discussion \u2014 Comment and organise with your club's members in complete privacy.

The federation is an open project: any club can join by following the technical protocol and the principles of transparency and privacy.

"}]

content.body:
[{"type": "rich_text", "value": "

La Nostra Storia

Fondato come piccolo gruppo di amici che si ritrovavano ogni domenica per una passeggiata sulle strade bergamasche, il club \u00e8 cresciuto fino a diventare uno dei pi\u00f9 attivi della Lombardia.

I Nostri Valori

  • Passione: La moto \u00e8 il nostro modo di vivere
  • Sicurezza: Guida responsabile e formazione continua
  • Solidariet\u00e0: Mutuo soccorso tra soci
  • Inclusivit\u00e0: Tutti i marchi e modelli sono benvenuti
", "id": "056fbc42-79ef-4c2d-870d-b825ca97b595"}, {"type": "timeline", "value": {"title": "Momenti Chiave", "items": [{"year": "1987", "title": "Fondazione", "description": "12 amici fondano il Moto Club Aquile Rosse a Bergamo."}, {"year": "1995", "title": "Affiliazione FMI", "description": "Il club si affilia alla Federazione Motociclistica Italiana."}, {"year": "2005", "title": "Nuova Sede", "description": "Inaugurata la nuova sede sociale in Via Borgo Palazzo."}, {"year": "2015", "title": "250 Soci", "description": "Il club raggiunge il traguardo di 250 soci attivi."}, {"year": "2024", "title": "Federazione", "description": "Avvio del programma di federazione con club partner in Italia."}]}, "id": "a53fc595-8de8-4c24-b086-c74215f6c7b9"}]
content.intro:
Il Moto Club Aquile Rosse nasce nel 1987 a Bergamo dall'idea di un gruppo di appassionati motociclisti che volevano condividere la passione per le due ruote in un contesto di amicizia e solidarietà.

Oggi il club conta oltre 250 soci iscritti, con una sede sociale in Via Borgo Palazzo 22, Bergamo. Siamo affiliati alla FMI (Federazione Motociclistica Italiana) e organizziamo ogni anno più di 50 eventi tra raduni, tour, giornate in pista e iniziative benefiche.

content.faq:
[{"type": "faq", "value": {"title": "Domande frequenti sulla Federazione", "items": [{"question": "Cos'\u00e8 la federazione dei club?", "answer": "

\u00c8 una rete volontaria di club che condividono eventi e iniziative attraverso un protocollo sicuro. Ogni club mantiene la propria completa autonomia.

"}, {"question": "Devo pagare per accedere agli eventi federati?", "answer": "

No. L'accesso alla lista degli eventi partner \u00e8 incluso nella tessera associativa. L'iscrizione a un evento partner segue le regole del club organizzatore.

"}, {"question": "I miei dati personali vengono condivisi?", "answer": "

No. La federazione scambia solo dati sugli eventi e conteggi aggregati anonimi. Nessun dato personale viene mai trasmesso ai club partner.

"}, {"question": "Come viene garantita la sicurezza?", "answer": "

Ogni club partner dispone di chiavi API univoche. Le comunicazioni avvengono tramite HTTPS con autenticazione HMAC. I dati vengono validati e sanificati prima dell'importazione.

"}, {"question": "Posso partecipare agli eventi di un club partner?", "answer": "

Puoi esprimere interesse dalla nostra piattaforma. Per l'iscrizione effettiva, segui le indicazioni sul sito del club organizzatore tramite il link nella pagina dell'evento.

"}, {"question": "I commenti sono visibili ai club partner?", "answer": "

No. I commenti sugli eventi partner sono visibili solo ai soci del nostro club. Sono uno strumento di discussione interna.

"}]}, "id": "235384eb-1397-47b5-9093-33b66fa88e15"}]
content.body:
[{"type": "rich_text", "value": "

Vantaggi della federazione

Far parte di una rete federata di club significa ampliare le opportunit\u00e0 per i soci senza rinunciare all'autonomia:

  • Pi\u00f9 eventi \u2014 Accedi a un calendario condiviso con decine di appuntamenti dai club partner.
  • Nuove amicizie \u2014 Conosci appassionati di altri club con interessi simili ai tuoi.
  • Privacy garantita \u2014 I tuoi dati personali restano nel nostro club.
  • Discussione riservata \u2014 Commenta e organizzati con i soci del tuo club in modo privato.

La federazione \u00e8 un progetto aperto: qualsiasi club pu\u00f2 aderire rispettando il protocollo tecnico e i principi di trasparenza e privacy.

", "id": "e951b29f-28fe-4535-a3b7-2d9ac2c97082"}]
content.intro: La nostra rete di club partner condivide eventi, iniziative e opportunità per tutti i soci. Grazie alla federazione puoi scoprire cosa succede nei club amici, esplorare i loro eventi e partecipare alle iniziative della rete — tutto dalla piattaforma del tuo club, in totale sicurezza.

content.body:
[{"type": "rich_text", "value": "

Come ogni anno, la stagione motociclistica si apre con il tradizionale Avviamento Motori a Mandello del Lario, la patria della Moto moto.

L'evento, organizzato dal Moto Club Le Aquile di Mandello prevede un programma ricco di attivit\u00e0: dalla sfilata lungo il lago alle visite guidate al Museo Moto moto, fino alla cena sociale presso il Ristorante Il Griso.

Programma

  • Ore 9:00 - Ritrovo presso Piazza Garibaldi
  • Ore 10:30 - Sfilata lungo il Lago di Como
  • Ore 12:30 - Pranzo sociale
  • Ore 15:00 - Visita al museo della moto
  • Ore 18:00 - Aperitivo e premiazioni

Il Moto Club Aquile Rosse parteciper\u00e0 con una delegazione di 30 soci. Le iscrizioni sono aperte fino al 15 marzo.

", "id": "31d18026-12d0-45d6-bd9f-212ddefd4548"}]
content.intro: Il tradizionale raduno di apertura stagione torna a Mandello del Lario con novità e sorprese per tutti gli appassionati di moto.
content.title: Avviamento Motori 2026: Si Riparte da Mandello!

fields.object_str: 1° Moto Lands of Pisa: Nuovo Evento in Toscana

content.body:
[{"type": "rich_text", "value": "

Grandi novit\u00e0 dal panorama dei raduni motociclistici italiani: \u00e8 stato annunciato il 1\u00b0 Moto moto Lands of Pisa, un evento che si terr\u00e0 a Pontedera (PI) nella primavera 2026.

L'evento \u00e8 organizzato dal Moto Club Terre di Pisa in collaborazione con la rete moto Days e promette un weekend all'insegna della cultura motociclistica toscana, con percorsi tra le colline pisane, degustazioni di prodotti locali e un raduno statico nel centro storico.

Il nostro club sta gi\u00e0 organizzando una trasferta di gruppo. Chi \u00e8 interessato pu\u00f2 contattare il responsabile eventi Davide Marchetti.

", "id": "8e3e3c48-92bb-4cd5-9888-bfd05677ed5f"}]
content.intro: Nasce un nuovo raduno motociclistico a Pontedera, nella terra dove nacque la Piaggio e a due passi dalla torre pendente.
content.title: 1° Moto Lands of Pisa: Nuovo Evento in Toscana

content.body:
[{"type": "rich_text", "value": "

Lo scorso weekend un gruppo di 8 soci del Moto Club Aquile Rosse ha attraversato le Alpi per partecipare al Spring Franken Bayern Treffen, il raduno primaverile che segna l'inizio della stagione nella regione tedesca della Franconia.

Il percorso di andata ha toccato il Passo del Brennero, Innsbruck e Monaco prima di arrivare nella zona di Norimberga. Il raduno, frequentato da oltre 500 motociclisti provenienti da tutta Europa, ha offerto giornate di cavalcate nelle splendide strade della Franconia, birra bavarese e un'atmosfera di grande cameratismo.

Prossima trasferta internazionale: il moto Days 2026 in Sudafrica!

", "id": "a7525a86-61ee-4353-b86c-ce0a4e1218ed"}]
content.intro: Otto soci del club hanno partecipato al raduno primaverile in Baviera. Ecco il racconto dell'esperienza.
content.title: Resoconto: Spring Franken Bayern Treffen 2026

content.body:

Siamo lieti di annunciare una nuova convenzione con Officina Moto Bergamo, centro assistenza multimarca situato in Via Seriate 42.

Tutti i soci del Moto Club Aquile Rosse potranno usufruire dei seguenti sconti:

  • 15% su tagliandi e manutenzione ordinaria
  • 10% su ricambi originali e aftermarket
  • Diagnosi elettronica gratuita
  • Priorit\u00e0 nelle prenotazioni durante l'alta stagione

Per accedere alle agevolazioni \u00e8 sufficiente presentare la tessera socio in corso di validit\u00e0.

fields.object_str: Guida alla Preparazione della Moto per la Stagione

content.body:
[{"type": "rich_text", "value": "

Con l'arrivo della bella stagione \u00e8 tempo di preparare la moto per i primi giri. Ecco una checklist essenziale preparata con il nostro partner tecnico Officina Moto Bergamo.

Controlli Essenziali

  1. Batteria: Verificare la carica e i livelli dell'elettrolito. Se la moto \u00e8 stata ferma pi\u00f9 di 3 mesi, considerare la sostituzione.
  2. Pneumatici: Controllare pressione, usura e data di fabbricazione (DOT). Pneumatici pi\u00f9 vecchi di 5 anni vanno sostituiti.
  3. Freni: Verificare lo spessore delle pastiglie e il livello del liquido freni.
  4. Olio motore: Cambiare olio e filtro se non fatto prima del rimessaggio.
  5. Catena: Pulire, lubrificare e verificare la tensione.
  6. Luci: Controllare tutte le luci, incluse frecce e luce targa.
", "id": "0cf37aa4-3851-440c-b579-84f384889898"}]
content.intro: I consigli del nostro meccanico di fiducia per rimettere la moto in forma dopo la pausa invernale.
content.title: Guida alla Preparazione della Moto per la Stagione

Si \u00e8 tenuta sabato scorso l'assemblea annuale dei soci del Moto Club Aquile Rosse presso la sede sociale.

Con la partecipazione di 142 soci su 258 aventi diritto, l'assemblea ha approvato all'unanimit\u00e0 il bilancio consuntivo 2025 e il bilancio preventivo 2026.

Punti Principali

  • Confermato il direttivo in carica per il biennio 2026-2027
  • Approvato il calendario eventi con 52 uscite programmate
  • Nuova partnership con 3 officine convenzionate
  • Lancio del sistema di federazione con altri club
  • Acquisto di un defibrillatore per la sede
", "id": "aaf5e221-4fa7-4164-9a5a-364b5e1f6825"}]
content.intro: L'assemblea annuale ha approvato all'unanimità il bilancio e il programma eventi per il nuovo anno.

Il Ride for Children \u00e8 l'evento benefico annuale del Moto Club Aquile Rosse, giunto alla sua 12\u00aa edizione.

L'intero ricavato delle iscrizioni sar\u00e0 devoluto al reparto di Pediatria dell'Ospedale Papa Giovanni XXIII di Bergamo per l'acquisto di attrezzature mediche.

Nelle edizioni precedenti abbiamo raccolto oltre \u20ac35.000 grazie alla generosit\u00e0 di centinaia di motociclisti.

Aperto a tutti, soci e non soci!

", "id": "8f59875f-3d85-49c3-baf4-762b56208ad9"}]

content.title: Beneficenza: Ride for Children
content.location_address: Piazza Vecchia, 24129 Bergamo
